mnemonics.title: NCC Key Properties
mnemonics.content: NCC: N ot C omplex C lassifier - stores only C entroids, uses C losest distance. Think 'Mean = Prototype'.

mnemonics.title: NCC Assumptions
mnemonics.content: LINEAR: Linearly separable, Independent features (not really but assumed), Normal-ish distributions (not required), Equal variance (spherical), All continuous.

mnemonics.title: Outlier Effect
mnemonics.content: NCC is S ENSITIVE to OUTLIERS - 'Nearest Centroid Can't Handle Outliers' - Mean gets pulled by extremes.

mnemonics.title: Shrinkage Purpose
mnemonics.content: Shrinkage PULLS centroids toward GLOBAL mean to PREVENT overfitting - think 'anchor to center'.

acronyms.usage: Simple classifier that uses class means for prediction
Alternative name for NCC emphasizing the use of mean vectors
NCC variant used in text/IR; computes ideal document prototypes

visualTips.visualization: Imagine two dots (centroids) on paper. The decision boundary is the straight line perpendicular to the line connecting them, passing through the midpoint. Points on one side go to one class.
Think of each class as a cloud of points. The centroid is like the 'center of mass' or 'balance point' - where the cloud would balance on a pin.
Picture class centroids as balloons. The global mean is an anchor. Shrinkage pulls the balloon strings toward the anchor, preventing them from drifting too far (overfitting).

analogies.analogy: NCC is like knowing the 'average customer' for each store type and sending new customers to the closest average. k-NN is like asking your nearest 5 neighbors which store they prefer.
The centroid is like the 'average height' in a group - one extremely tall person can drastically change the average. That's why outliers hurt NCC.
Two classmates have average scores. The 'decision boundary' is like the score exactly midway between their averages - anyone above goes to one, below to the other.
